meta.title: General Physiology I — Predicted Exam Questions (One-offs)

behaviour.mcq: Select one option. Answer revealed immediately on selection.

questions.stem: Glucose enters nerve cells by:

options.B: Simple diffusion through the lipid bilayer
options.D: Facilitated diffusion

questions.stem: The resting membrane potential of nerve cells ranges between:

options.D: -65 mV and -90 mV

questions.stem: Polarization of the cell membrane is:

options.A: The difference in Na+ concentration between intracellular and extracellular fluid
options.B: The difference in electrical potential between the inside and outside of the cell membrane
options.C: The difference in osmotic pressure between the inside and outside of the cell
options.D: The difference in protein concentration between intracellular and extracellular fluid

questions.stem: When the Na+ channel is 'open' (activated):

options.A: The m gate and the h gate are both open
options.B: The m gate and h gate are both half-open
options.C: The m gate is open but the h gate is closed
options.D: The h gate is open but the m gate is closed

questions.stem: Which statement is NOT true regarding the all-or-none response?

options.A: A threshold stimulus must act for it to occur
options.B: Conduction of the impulse depends on the type of neuron
options.C: Conduction of the impulse does not depend on stimulus intensity
options.D: The phenomenon is observed only in peripheral nerves

questions.stem: Neurotransmitter release into the synaptic cleft is:

options.A: Controlled by neuronal influx of calcium ions
options.B: Controlled by neuronal influx of sodium ions
options.D: Controlled by neuronal influx of potassium ions

questions.stem: Acetylcholine is a neurotransmitter at:

options.D: Preganglionic parasympathetic fibers, postganglionic parasympathetic fibers, and neuromuscular junctions

questions.stem: The 'H' zone of striated muscle consists of:

options.D: Myosin only

questions.stem: A transverse section of a skeletal muscle fiber through the H zone would reveal:

options.D: Actin, myosin, and titin

questions.stem: The Z disc of the sarcomere:

options.B: Does not move during muscle contraction
options.D: Consists of actin and myosin filaments

questions.stem: During contraction of striated muscle:

options.B: The width of the A band decreases
options.D: The distance between Z lines increases

questions.stem: Detachment of actin from the myosin cross-bridges during contraction:

options.B: Occurs when Ca2+ binds to troponin
options.C: Depends on the release of ACh at the synapse
options.D: Requires binding of ATP to the myosin head

questions.stem: Tropomyosin:

options.A: Acts as the Ca2+ binding site during contraction
options.C: Prevents the binding of actin to myosin at rest
options.D: Is a protein of the thick filaments

questions.stem: In the contraction mechanism of smooth muscle fibers, Ca2+ binds to:

options.D: Myosin

questions.stem: The force of contraction a muscle can produce isometrically:

options.C: Is maximal at resting length and decreases at greater or smaller lengths
options.D: Is independent of muscle length

questions.stem: The sarcoplasmic reticulum:

options.A: Releases Ca2+ to initiate contraction and actively removes Ca2+ to achieve relaxation
options.B: Conducts action potentials into the interior of the cell
options.C: Only actively removes Ca2+ from the sarcoplasm
options.D: Only releases Ca2+ to initiate contraction

questions.stem: Smooth muscle cells begin to contract by all of the following mechanisms EXCEPT:

options.B: Norepinephrine binding to alpha-1 adrenergic receptors
options.C: Movement of a food bolus in the gastrointestinal tract
options.D: Excitation spreading from a neighboring cell through gap junctions

questions.stem: The lifespan of red blood cells is approximately:
Erythropoiesis:

options.C: Occurs mainly in the liver after age 20
options.D: Is normal when reticulocytes are 50% of red cells

questions.stem: A hematocrit of 41% means:

options.A: 41% of the total blood volume is plasma
options.B: 41% of the blood volume consists of red blood cells
options.D: 41% of hemoglobin is in the plasma

questions.stem: Individuals with sickle-cell anemia have:

options.D: HbS

questions.stem: Tissue macrophages originate from:

options.D: Monocytes

questions.stem: Eosinophils:

options.A: Have no phagocytic ability at all
options.D: Do not contain granules

questions.stem: The normal proportion of polymorphonuclear (neutrophil) leukocytes among total leukocytes in adult peripheral blood is:
Natural killer (NK) cells of the immune system:

options.D: Are cytotoxic lymphocytes that induce apoptosis in their targets (all of these)

questions.stem: Which of the following cell types is NOT part of the reticuloendothelial system?

options.D: Purkinje cells

questions.stem: The sequence of events in the primary hemostatic plug is:

options.A: Platelet adhesion, aggregation, and release of substances
options.D: Platelet adhesion and fibrin formation
